titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 560

p.seg: Edgar by the grace of God king of the Scots to all to whom the present letters shall have come, as much to Frenchmen and English as Gaelic, greeting.

us, from the licence of William the king of England, overlord of the kingdom of Scotland, for the sake of the souls of Malcolm our father and Margaret our mother, and for the souls of Edward and Duncan our brothers, and also for the sake of our own soul, to have given and conceded to God and Saint Cuthbert the confessor, to William the bishop, and Turgot the prior, and to the monks of Durham serving God in perpetuity, the barony and manor of Coldingham, with these manors written below, to wit, Berwick, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Reston, 'Faudon', Ayton, the other Ayton, Prederguest, Cramsmoo, Graden, Lennel, 'Dilchestre', Edrom, Chirnside, Hilton, Blackadder, Hutton, Renton, Paxton, 'Fulden', Mordington, Lamberton, the other Lamberton, Edrington, Horford, and Upsettlington, with all pertinents, rights, and teinds of wheat and grain crops and hay, with all liberties and royal immunities as freely and fully as our aforesaid father and lord held it. For the aforesaid bishop, prior, and monks and their successors to have and to hold barony and manor, dwellings and teinds, all in general and in particular, with all liberties, royal immunities, and rights whatsoever, of us and our heirs in pure and perpetual alms for ever. And we and our heirs shall protect and defend for the bishop, prior, monks, and their successors, the aforesaid barony and manor, dwellings and teinds, all in general and particular, with all liberties, royal immunities and rights whatsoever, as it has been said before, against all people.
In testimony of this this matter we have caused our seal to be applied to the present letters at Norham

in the presence of the aforesaid King William of England the overlord of the kingdom of Scotland, and of the aforesaid bishop and prior, of Walter de Valognes, Odinel de Umfraville, Racho de Loreyns, Odinel Heron, Robert de Mandeville, and a great multitude of French, English and Scots.
